description: DEBATE IN THE SENATE Monday April 30 1838 INDIAN TERRITORYConcluded The Senate having under consideration the bill to pro vide for the security and protection of the emigrant and other Indians west of Missouri and Arkansas and the question being on Mr Kings motion to amend the bill so as to reserve all that portion of country belonging to the United States to which the Indian title had not been ex tinguished lying east of the Rocky Mountains north of the Missouri river north and west of the State of Mis souri and west of the Mississippi river to constitute an Indian Territory to be secured to the Indians and their de scendants forever Mr KING said he had no disposition if his health per mitted to consume much of the time of the Senate in sup port of the amendment and he was the less disposed to do so as he was fully aware what little hopes there were of its success He regretted the temper of the Senate on this subject and could not but feel surprised that an amend ment so important to the frontier States of the Southwest should not meet with a more favorable consideration
